title: Direct Routing and Bounded Worker Pools · Language Lens: Competing Consumer Semantics
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must combine direct routing with bounded goroutines, prefetch, dependency capacity, and acknowledgement after durable effect; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the competing consumer semantics increment in a capacity-aware Go work distribution service. Learners…
objectives: Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Competing Consumer Semantics · Theory Competing Consumer Semantics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 6859 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Competing Consumer Semantics contract for the supplied data.
